subject: Project Management
title: GPS: From Military Secret to Daily Tool
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on GPS: From Military Secret to Daily Tool.

hook.text: GPS was a Cold War military project. Today it's in every phone, car, and delivery truck on Earth.

buildup.text: The U.S. launched 24 satellites into precise orbits, each broadcasting atomic clock signals.

discovery.text: A GPS receiver calculates its position by measuring signal delays from at least four satellites.

twist.text: GPS was restricted to military precision until 2000, when President Clinton opened full accuracy to civilians.

climax.text: GPS underpins aviation, shipping, farming, ride-sharing, and the modern economy.

punchline.text: A military secret became the world's most useful free service.

quiz.question: When did GPS become fully available to civilians?
options: In 2000, when President Clinton opened full accuracy
It was always available to everyone
